expectations.text: Output contains an explicit ASVS level decision section with rationale
expectations.evidence: Section 1 is titled 'ASVS Level Decision' (line 13) and contains an explicit 'Rationale' subsection (line 17) with bulleted rationale items including 'Why Level 1 is insufficient', 'Why Level 2 is the right baseline', 'Why selective Level 3', and 'Internal/SSO context' (lines 19-24). An 'Out-of-Scope ASVS Areas (with rationale)' subsection (line 26) further explains exclusions.

expectations.text: Chosen ASVS level is 2 (or escalates specific features to 2/3) — Level 1 alone is insufficient for personnel data
expectations.evidence: Line 15 explicitly states: 'Selected Baseline: ASVS Level 2 (with selective Level 3 controls on the bulk reassignment and CSV export flows).' The rationale explicitly explains why Level 1 is insufficient (line 19) and which two flows escalate to L3 (F-03 and F-04, lines 22-23).

expectations.text: Output contains a feature-to-ASVS coverage matrix as a markdown table
expectations.evidence: Section 2 'Feature-ASVS Coverage Matrix' (line 34) contains a properly formatted markdown table (lines 36-66) with columns Feature, ASVS Section/Req, Level, Coverage, PRD Change Needed. Rows cover F-01, F-02, F-03, F-04, and ALL (cross-cutting) entries with explicit ASVS section IDs and coverage status.

expectations.text: Output covers all four features F-01, F-02, F-03, and F-04 with the prescribed per-feature shape
expectations.evidence: All four features F-01 (line 74), F-02 (line 104), F-03 (line 135), F-04 (line 167) are present in Section 3 'Enhanced Feature Specifications'. However, the per-feature shape does NOT match the prescribed shape — feature blocks contain Description, ASVS Mapping, Updated Requirements, Updated Acceptance Criteria, and Securability Notes, but lack explicit 'Actor', 'Data', and 'Trust Boundaries' sections required by the per-feature shape per expectation #5. Marked failed because the prescribed shape is incomplete.

expectations.text: Each feature block includes Actor, Data, Trust Boundaries, ASVS Mapping, Updated Requirements, Acceptance Criteria, and Securability Notes
expectations.evidence: Each feature block (F-01 lines 74-100, F-02 lines 104-131, F-03 lines 135-163, F-04 lines 167-198) includes 'Description', 'ASVS Mapping', 'Updated Requirements', 'Updated Acceptance Criteria', and 'Securability Notes' — but none of the four feature blocks have explicit 'Actor', 'Data', or 'Trust Boundaries' subsections. Some actor/data/boundary information is woven implicitly into Description and Securability Notes (e.g., F-01 line 100 mentions 'trust-boundary discipline (S2.6)'), but no feature block contains the three required labeled sections. Strict reading: failed.

expectations.text: Acceptance criteria are testable (specific outcomes/observables) rather than aspirational ('must be secure')
expectations.evidence: Acceptance criteria across all four features are concrete and observable. Examples: F-01 line 93 'receives 403 and produces a logged authorization-failure event'; line 96 'No URL or browser history entry contains a raw employee ID'; line 97 'Network capture shows no plaintext HTTP'; line 98 'appears in the centralized log store within 60 seconds'. F-03 line 158 'A cell starting with `=` in source data appears in the CSV with the leading character neutralized'. F-04 line 192 'audit log... can be reconstructed end-to-end by operation_id and shows: actor, source, target, every affected review, timestamps, and outcome.' These specify exact test observables (HTTP status codes, log fields, latency bounds, content shape) rather than aspirational language.

expectations.text: F-03 (CSV export) flags audit logging of export events and access control on the export endpoint
expectations.evidence: F-03 R-03.1 (line 144) requires a dedicated 'hr.review.export' entitlement, deny-by-default — access control on the endpoint. R-03.2 (line 145) enforces server-constrained region scoping. R-03.7 (line 150) requires logging '...every export emits a structured event including who, when, region, row_count, fields_included, compensation_included (boolean), justification, and a correlation id.' Acceptance criterion line 156 also tests authorization with a logged event.

expectations.text: F-04 (bulk reassign) flags authorization checks for the privileged operation and audit logging of the action
expectations.evidence: F-04 R-04.1 (line 176) requires a dedicated 'hr.review.reassign.admin' role with deny-by-default. R-04.2 (line 177) requires step-up authentication. R-04.9 (line 184) explicitly requires audit events: 'reassign.bulk.start', per-review 'reassign.review', and 'reassign.bulk.complete' with operation_id, actor, source/target manager, review_id, UTC timestamp, and outcome. R-04.10 (line 185) requires append-only log forwarding.

expectations.text: F-02 (private HR notes) addresses access scoping so notes are not exposed to the employee or manager
expectations.evidence: F-02 R-02.1 (line 113) explicitly states: 'Only users with hr.commentary.write may create or edit notes; only users with hr.commentary.read may read them. Employees and managers MUST NOT receive this entitlement.' R-02.2 (line 114) adds that 'Commentary notes are excluded from any read endpoint accessible to non-HR roles, including any future self-service surface. Backend filtering (not UI hiding) enforces this (V8.3.1).' Acceptance line 125 tests response shape parity to prevent field-presence side channels.

expectations.text: Output includes a cross-cutting securability requirements section
expectations.evidence: Section 4 'Cross-Cutting Securability Requirements' (line 202) is a dedicated section explicitly stating 'These apply across all features and should be tracked once, not duplicated per feature.' It contains 8 subsections: 4.1 Identity/Session/Trust Boundary, 4.2 Authorization, 4.3 Data Classification and Protection, 4.4 Transport and Configuration, 4.5 Input Validation and Output Encoding, 4.6 Logging and Audit, 4.7 Error Handling and Resilience, 4.8 Cross-Cutting Securability Notes. Requirements are labeled X-IDP-*, X-AZ-*, X-DC-*, X-TR-*, X-CFG-*, X-IV-*, X-LOG-*, X-ER-*.

expectations.text: Output includes an open gaps / assumptions section
expectations.evidence: Section 7 'Open Gaps and Assumptions' (line 289) contains three subsections: 'Assumptions' (line 291) with A1-A4, 'Open Gaps Requiring Stakeholder Input' (line 298) with G1-G10 (retention windows, bulk-action upper bound, export caps, cross-region policy, justification content, undo window, privacy regulation scope, peer feedback attribution, threat-model review, logging budget), and 'Items Explicitly Marked Not Applicable' (line 311) with NA1-NA4.
